title: Humboltia retusa
taxonomy: Humboltia retusa is a member of the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family comprises a diverse range of flowering plants, including some species that are toxic to humans and animals. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into various subfamilies and tribes, each containing unique characteristics and species. As a member of this family, Humboltia retusa is likely to share some common traits with other Apocynaceae species. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships within the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Humboldtia retusa is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions.
